text:
In the quiet town of Elmwood woods lived Willow, a magical willow tree. Willow knew everything that happened around her, could talk, sing birdsongs, and was often sought after for advice. She noticed the townsfolk growing quieter. Something was wrong.

Willow decided to investigate. Her branches whipped through the air, casting sparkling rays into the forest. She found a group of children on the edge of the woods. They looked scared.

"Why are you so sad?" Willow called.

"I don’t know," they admitted. "We don’t even know who we’re going to scare."

"What could you scare them with?"

"Well.... sometimes we just..." they looked at each other nervously.

Willow smiled knowingly.

"Sometimes, it's the things we think we know that frighten us," she said softly. "What really frightens you?"

"I think it’s the thing we’re scared of most," one boy whispered.

"What?”

"A monster!”
